mnemonics: TEST DRIVE (Test Component, Design, Review, Implement, Verify, Execute) - The process for creating and running a test.
A-B-C (Arrange, Behavior, Check) - The basic structure of a unit test.
keyPoints: A test component is a piece of code written specifically to verify the correctness of another piece of code (the data structure implementation).
Key testing types include: Unit Tests (test individual units/methods), Integration Tests (test how units work together), and Regression Tests (ensure new changes don't break old functionality).
Good tests are independent, repeatable, and cover both typical use cases (happy path) and edge cases (empty structures, invalid inputs, boundary conditions).
commonMistakes: Testing only the 'happy path' and forgetting to test edge cases and error conditions (e.g., popping from an empty stack, inserting a null value).
Writing tests that are not independent, where the success of one test relies on the state left by a previous test, leading to unreliable results.
